run.label: hard_catalog_locked_nominal_recovery
run.evidence_level: hard catalog-DRO locked-nominal continuous branch-recovery diagnostic baseline; not quantum evidence

cases.purpose: Hard catalog-DRO feasible locked nominal trajectory; selected_worst_error equals nominal_error by construction.
Hard catalog-DRO locked-nominal recovery for the single hardest eligible missed-thrust mask.
Hard catalog-DRO locked-nominal recovery for the three hardest eligible missed-thrust masks.
Hard catalog-DRO locked-nominal recovery for the 8 hardest one-segment masks with at least six remaining recovery segments; explicitly not late-outage or two-segment robustness.
Hard catalog-DRO locked-nominal recovery for all one-segment missed-thrust masks; explicitly not two-segment robustness.

M data/results/catalog_collocation_feasibility/multiple_shooting_feasibility.csv

?? tests/test_locked_nominal_recovery.py
threshold_rule: meets_thresholds requires nominal_error <= nominal_success and selected_worst_error <= robust_success

semantics.backend: Locked-nominal independent branch recovery is a diagnostic continuous backend baseline, not quantum evidence. It first solves a nominal all-windows multiple-shooting trajectory, then freezes that nominal control sequence.
semantics.branch_recovery: Each selected missed-thrust mask is optimized independently. Pre-recovery controls are fixed to locked nominal controls with the missed segment(s) zeroed by the mask; only post-outage recovery controls are variables.
semantics.selection: Integer selected_outages policies choose hardest masks by fixed nominal masked-control terminal error. all_single selects all one-segment masks; all/all_configured selects every configured mask.
semantics.all_mask: all_mask_worst_error is diagnostic across all configured masks; selected masks use optimized branches and unselected masks use masked locked nominal controls only.
semantics.resume: resume reuses only rows whose suite_case_id, settings_fingerprint, config_hash, and source_states_id match current settings

limitations: This baseline is not integrated into the manuscript by this script.
It does not prove universal all-mask robustness unless all configured masks are selected.
Optimizer failure flags are retained even when threshold metrics are met.

rows.purpose: Hard catalog-DRO feasible locked nominal trajectory; selected_worst_error equals nominal_error by construction.
rows.target_generation: ballistic propagation of target_dro_seed for transfer_time
rows.backend_semantics: Locked-nominal continuous baseline: nominal all-windows controls are solved once, kept fixed, and each selected missed-thrust recovery branch is optimized independently after the outage.
rows.selection_semantics: selected_outages=0; no branch recovery optimizer is run
rows.selected_branch_semantics: For selected masks, controls before outage_end(mask) are exactly locked nominal controls with missed segments zeroed; only controls from outage_end(mask) onward are optimization variables.
rows.all_mask_diagnostic_semantics: all_outage_errors evaluates every configured outage mask; selected masks use their optimized recovery branches, while unselected masks use masked locked nominal controls only.
rows.control_bound_semantics: Every nominal and recovery acceleration vector is projected to the Euclidean ball ||u_i|| <= amax before propagation, residual evaluation, fuel computation, and reporting.
rows.nominal_lock_semantics: Branch optimization never changes nominal_controls; nominal_error is the locked nominal baseline terminal error and nominal_lock_error_delta reports agreement with the nominal solve output.
rows.worst_error_semantics: selected_worst_error is the worst terminal error among independently optimized selected locked-nominal branches
